title: SAT Math: Systems of Equations — Elimination Method
description:
Mastering Systems of Equations is essential for the SAT Heart of Algebra section. This problem shows how to use the Elimination Method — one of the fastest approaches on the SAT.

📐 Topic: Systems of Linear Equations (Heart of Algebra)
⏱️ Difficulty: Medium
📝 SAT Section: Math (Calculator & No-Calculator)

In this video:
✅ Set up the system
✅ Eliminate one variable by adding equations
✅ Solve and back-substitute
✅ Verify the solution

🔗 Free SAT practice: https://mathhub.io/us/sat/

Timestamps:
0:00 Problem statement
0:15 Step 1: Line up equations for elimination
0:40 Step 2: Add to eliminate y
1:05 Step 3: Back-substitute to find y
1:30 Answer reveal
1:45 Key takeaway

#SAT #SATMath #SystemsOfEquations #Elimination #SATPrep #HeartOfAlgebra #Algebra

slides.text:
$2x + y = 11$
$x - y = 4$

What is the value of $x$?
slides.note: Hint: Look for a way to cancel one variable!

slides.heading: Add the two equations
slides.explanation: Notice $+y$ and $-y$ cancel each other. Add the equations:
Check: $2(5)+1=11$ ✓ and $5-1=4$ ✓  Elimination works when coefficients of one variable are opposites — or can be made opposite by multiplying.
